unit: % marginal rate on offshore petroleum profit

fields.state_participation: SDFI (State's Direct Financial Interest) — the Norwegian state holds direct equity stakes in petroleum licences and infrastructure, separately from Equinor which is majority state-owned.
fields.sovereign_wealth_fund: Government Pension Fund Global (GPFG) — petroleum revenues net of state spending are transferred to the fund; the fiscal rule caps annual withdrawals at the fund's expected real return.
fields.benchmark_only_caveat: This is a benchmark, not a claim of structural equivalence. Norway is a smaller economy with concentrated offshore production under a single national fiscal regime; Australia has a federal PRRT on a subset of projects plus separate state royalty regimes on onshore and state-waters production.

notes: Norway applies a 22% ordinary corporate income tax plus a 71.8% special petroleum tax to offshore petroleum profits, giving a combined marginal rate of 78%. State participation operates through the SDFI and majority state ownership of Equinor. Petroleum revenues flow to the Government Pension Fund Global under the fiscal rule. Primary source: Norwegian Petroleum Directorate / Ministry of Petroleum and Energy at norskpetroleum.no.
citation: Norwegian Petroleum Directorate and Ministry of Petroleum and Energy, 'The government's revenues' and 'Norway's petroleum tax system', norskpetroleum.no, retrieved 21 April 2026.
